user_query:
I’m a baseball nut and I want a simple, reproducible local publishing setup for my recap articles so I can relive big moments every week. Please turn this repo into a small, self-contained static site pipeline using only Python 3’s standard library, and update my docs and messaging accordingly. Inputs to use: - Config: config.yaml (root) - Articles: articles/*.md (with YAML front matter for title and date) - Docs to edit: README.md and DEPLOYMENT.md (both currently outdated) - Draft message: newsletter_draft.txt Deliverables and constraints: 1) Build script (tools/build_site.py): - When run from the repo root (python tools/build_site.py), read config.yaml for site_title and process every Markdown file in articles/ into HTML files in public/ with the same base name (e.g., articles/2023-10-28-walkoff.md -> public/2023-10-28-walkoff.html). - Parse the YAML front matter delimited by lines of "---" for at least title and date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD). Do not require external libraries; use only the Python standard library and simple parsing. - Generate minimal, readable HTML using a consistent template. Each article page must include: - A {article title} | {site_title} - The site title from config.yaml clearly visible in the page header. - The article title as a visible H1 and the article date in ISO format inside a element. - Body content that at least supports converting lines starting with "# " to

, "## " to

, and other non-blank lines into paragraphs (

). (You may ignore other Markdown features.) - Create public/index.html with a reverse-chronological list (newest first) of all articles. Each list item must include the article title, the date, and a working relative link to its generated HTML file. 2) Local serve script (scripts/serve_local.sh): - A small shell script that starts a simple local server rooted at public/ using Python’s http.server on port 8000. 3) Edit and rewrite the existing docs in place: - Update README.md to remove the outdated Node/"dist" instructions and document the new Python build and local-serve steps (including the expected Python version, how to run tools/build_site.py, and how to use scripts/serve_local.sh). Keep it concise and clear. - Update DEPLOYMENT.md to reflect the new build output path (public/) and include brief, clear steps for building and copying the public/ directory to any static host (no provider specifics required). Replace references to the old Node workflow. 4) Run the build so outputs exist locally: - Produce the generated site in public/ based on the current articles so I can open public/index.html and the article pages. 5) Compose a brief status summary: - Create out/STATUS.md that includes: a short summary of what you configured, the list of generated files under public/ (one per line, relative paths), and any assumptions you made. 6) Rewrite the newsletter: - Rewrite newsletter_draft.txt to out/newsletter_final.txt with a warm, enthusiastic tone and under 120 words. Make it clearer and more concise, mention that new recaps are live locally, and include a simple call to action to check out the latest posts. Notes: - Do not add third-party dependencies or require Internet access; rely only on the Python standard library. - Keep paths exactly as specified so I can verify them locally. - All changes to README.md and DEPLOYMENT.md must be edits to the existing files, not replacements in new locations. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
